1	# REWRITTEN RACE PLAN (general, weight-based, §-1.3-compliant) — for gate review
2	
3	## Governing rule every item is held to (CLAUDE.md §-1.3, operator-locked)
4	- WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER. Every relevant source flows through with a credibility weight; nothing hard-dropped
5	  to hit a number.
6	- CONSOLIDATE, DON'T DROP. Same-claim sources form a basket; corroboration is the point.
7	- The FAITHFULNESS ENGINE is the ONLY hard gate. Everything else is a weight or a render choice.
8	- PROMPT-GOVERNED & GENERAL. Every behavior reads the QUESTION'S OWN WORDS and works across all ~100 tasks —
9	  zero task literals, zero hardcoded venue/industry/journal lists.
10	- SURGICAL. Re-wire machinery that already exists; do not rebuild; do not bolt on target-forcing knobs.
11	  BANNED (§-1.3): "If you find yourself adding a knob to make a number hit a target — STOP."
12	
13	## Overfit items REMOVED from the prior plan (transparency)
14	- "name the journal in prose" -> hardcodes journal. GENERALIZED: narrative source attribution (author/venue/
15	  year) driven by the EXISTING tier/weight system, for any question.
16	- "enforce journal-only" -> task-72 literal. GENERALIZED: parse WHATEVER source/scope constraints the prompt
17	  states; fold into the existing credibility weight.
18	- "raise the section word-count target" -> number-forcing target (§-1.3 bans this). GENERALIZED: remove the
19	  artificial "write one paragraph" cap; density EMERGES from real synthesis.
20	- "add tables to lift Readability" -> forcing an artifact to hit a number. GENERALIZED: tables EMERGE when the
21	  evidence is genuinely comparable or the prompt asks for one.
22	- Sol's "no source-type-mismatch candidate enters the corpus" -> a hard filter; §-1.3.1 only permits deleting
23	  chrome + semantic-off-topic. GENERALIZED: weight, never drop.
24	
25	## Why the run was flat (agreed by all four passes)
26	The judge strips our [N] markers before scoring, so our prose reads UNCITED; the reference's narrative citations
27	survive. The cleaner KEEPS tables/bullets/bold — the reference has many, we have zero. The machinery that could
28	have helped NEVER FIRED: source-scope weighting isn't wired on this path; K3 is throttled by a one-line registry
29	bug that truncates every table; the artifact producers were off or unwired.
30	
31	## STEP 1 — Make measurement honest (first; general; cheap)
32	- 3 INDEPENDENT generator draws per version, not one draw scored 3x.
33	- Noise rule: <=0.007 = noise; real only if replicated mean clears ~+0.014.
34	- Write resolved flag/lever states into compose_summary.json every run (never "measure" a silently-inert lever
35	  again).
36	- Single-source the route-all setting (config off vs shell on — reconcile).
37	
38	## STEP 2 — Fix K3 as a first-class reasoning model (capability plumbing; general, not a target)
39	- Register kimi-k3 in the reasoning-model registry so its reasoning budget is actually transmitted (currently
40	  it is not — the one-line bug). General: ANY reasoning-first model must be registered; a transmission fix.
41	- Stop the driver passing a tiny hardcoded token cap; give table/section calls enough completion budget not to
42	  truncate; RETRY a truncated call instead of shipping nothing.
43	- Do NOT globally max K3 — only unblock the calls that truncate.
44	
45	## STEP 3 — Let structure and tables EMERGE (general, data-shape / prompt driven — never forced)
46	- Turn on the existing section-structure path so a section renders sub-headings/bullets/comparison table WHEN
47	  ITS OWN EVIDENCE SUPPORTS IT (e.g. >=3 sources on the same construct) — emergence from data shape, not a
48	  forced artifact. Each unit keeps its citation; faithfulness engine untouched.
49	- Build comparison tables DETERMINISTICALLY from comparable facts (group sources quantifying the same construct
50	  and render) — don't ask the model to invent Markdown then discard rows. Non-comparable findings stay in prose.
51	- Wire the already-built, PROMPT-READING summary-table renderer into compose — it parses whatever structured
52	  output the prompt asks for; general, deterministic, currently just unwired.
53	- Before any live run, REPLAY-VALIDATE the markdown survives the resolver flatten (provenance_generator.py:5121).
54	- Behavioral proof: if comparable data exists, the CLEANED article must actually contain a table with rows —
55	  assert it (no "flag enabled" surrogate).
56	- Remove the leftover "write one paragraph" instruction that contradicts structure.
57	
58	## STEP 4 — Carry source credibility in the PROSE (general; survives the cleaner)
59	- Because the cleaner deletes [N] markers, credibility must ride in the prose as NARRATIVE ATTRIBUTION (author,
60	  venue, year). Normal scholarly writing, applied to ANY question.
61	- The EXISTING tier/authority weight decides prominence — higher-weight source gets a named narrative
62	  attribution; low-weight still used, just not foregrounded. No "journal" literal; the writer names each
63	  source's ACTUAL venue (journal, agency, dataset, etc.).
64	
65	## STEP 5 — Let the prompt's scope govern WEIGHTING (general; weight, never filter)
66	- Parse the question's own scope words (source-type, language, recency, domain — WHATEVER it says) with the
67	  general extractor (empty -> no-op, so unconstrained questions untouched).
68	- Fold into the existing credibility weight so constraint-matching sources rank higher and fill the citation
69	  menu first. Off-constraint sources STAY at low weight — demote, never drop (§-1.3 principle 1).
70	- For LIVE fetches, bias query generation + backend choice toward the prompt's stated source-type — a RANKING
71	  bias within the existing fetch budget, not a new hard filter.
72	
73	## STEP 6 — Denser synthesis by USING THE BASKETS, not by targeting a length (general; the real +0.02 lever)
74	- Remove the artificial length cap; do NOT add a word-count target (banned day-waster).
75	- Instruct genuine cross-source synthesis: compose each claim from its WHOLE BASKET — surface where sources
76	  agree, disagree, and WHY (method/context) — the CONSOLIDATE principle expressed in prose. Density/depth EMERGE.
77	- Feed the model focused per-claim/per-facet evidence (grouped by the prompt's own facets) instead of dumping
78	  the whole corpus into one call. Better attention, general.
79	- The only lever the models agree can credibly clear +0.02 (Insight 0.32 + Comprehensiveness 0.29 live here).
80	
81	## STEP 7 — Prove causality and generality
82	- Isolation arms: current / K3+prompt-only / artifacts-only / scope-weighting-only / full package.
83	- Generality test: questions with hard, soft, mixed, and NO scope constraints — confirm never over-weight an
84	  unconstrained prompt; every decision traces to the parsed prompt, not a task ID.
85	
86	## Honest magnitude
87	Steps 2-5 individually sub-noise to small (~+0.005-0.015, overlapping). Step 6 (basket-driven synthesis +
88	weighted evidence + emergent tables together) = the only credible +0.02-0.03. Ceiling with the full package
89	~0.52-0.54; leaders ~0.55. Only counted if it survives the 3-draw test.
90	
91	## NOT DOING (incl. §-1.3 bans)
92	No entailment/faith-ghost/post-generation content-drop. No hard filters/caps/targets/thinners to move a number
93	(faithfulness engine is the sole hard gate). No hardcoded venue/journal/industry/task literals. No dropping
94	credible on-topic sources (only chrome + semantic-off-topic, disclosed). No charts/images (invisible to the
95	text scorer). No globally maxing K3. No trusting single-draw or "flag-enabled = fired".
96

  ITS OWN EVIDENCE SUPPORTS IT (e.g. >=3 sources on the same construct) — emergence from data shape, not a
  forced artifact. Each unit keeps its citation; faithfulness engine untouched.
- Build comparison tables DETERMINISTICALLY from comparable facts (group sources quantifying the same construct
  and render) — don't ask the model to invent Markdown then discard rows. Non-comparable findings stay in prose.
- Wire the already-built, PROMPT-READING summary-table renderer into compose — it parses whatever structured
  output the prompt asks for; general, deterministic, currently just unwired.
- Before any live run, REPLAY-VALIDATE the markdown survives the resolver flatten (provenance_generator.py:5121).
- Behavioral proof: if comparable data exists, the CLEANED article must actually contain a table with rows —
  assert it (no "flag enabled" surrogate).
- Remove the leftover "write one paragraph" instruction that contradicts structure.

## STEP 4 — Carry source credibility in the PROSE (general; survives the cleaner)
- Because the cleaner deletes [N] markers, credibility must ride in the prose as NARRATIVE ATTRIBUTION (author,
  venue, year). Normal scholarly writing, applied to ANY question.
- The EXISTING tier/authority weight decides prominence — higher-weight source gets a named narrative
  attribution; low-weight still used, just not foregrounded. No "journal" literal; the writer names each
  source's ACTUAL venue (journal, agency, dataset, etc.).

## STEP 5 — Let the prompt's scope govern WEIGHTING (general; weight, never filter)
- Parse the question's own scope words (source-type, language, recency, domain — WHATEVER it says) with the
  general extractor (empty -> no-op, so unconstrained questions untouched).
- Fold into the existing credibility weight so constraint-matching sources rank higher and fill the citation
  menu first. Off-constraint sources STAY at low weight — demote, never drop (§-1.3 principle 1).
- For LIVE fetches, bias query generation + backend choice toward the prompt's stated source-type — a RANKING
  bias within the existing fetch budget, not a new hard filter.

## STEP 6 — Denser synthesis by USING THE BASKETS, not by targeting a length (general; the real +0.02 lever)
- Remove the artificial length cap; do NOT add a word-count target (banned day-waster).
- Instruct genuine cross-source synthesis: compose each claim from its WHOLE BASKET — surface where sources
  agree, disagree, and WHY (method/context) — the CONSOLIDATE principle expressed in prose. Density/depth EMERGE.
- Feed the model focused per-claim/per-facet evidence (grouped by the prompt's own facets) instead of dumping
  the whole corpus into one call. Better attention, general.
- The only lever the models agree can credibly clear +0.02 (Insight 0.32 + Comprehensiveness 0.29 live here).

## STEP 7 — Prove causality and generality
- Isolation arms: current / K3+prompt-only / artifacts-only / scope-weighting-only / full package.
- Generality test: questions with hard, soft, mixed, and NO scope constraints — confirm never over-weight an
  unconstrained prompt; every decision traces to the parsed prompt, not a task ID.

## Honest magnitude
Steps 2-5 individually sub-noise to small (~+0.005-0.015, overlapping). Step 6 (basket-driven synthesis +
weighted evidence + emergent tables together) = the only credible +0.02-0.03. Ceiling with the full package
~0.52-0.54; leaders ~0.55. Only counted if it survives the 3-draw test.

## NOT DOING (incl. §-1.3 bans)
No entailment/faith-ghost/post-generation content-drop. No hard filters/caps/targets/thinners to move a number
(faithfulness engine is the sole hard gate). No hardcoded venue/journal/industry/task literals. No dropping
credible on-topic sources (only chrome + semantic-off-topic, disclosed). No charts/images (invisible to the
text scorer). No globally maxing K3. No trusting single-draw or "flag-enabled = fired".

18	   - BUT the module's own docstring (`:12-13`) states: *"Standalone module. Nothing here is wired into the driver or generator; the caller decides what (if anything) to do with the returned dict."*

Nothing in the compose driver or generator calls it. So the extraction exists but its output is never consumed on this run path.
20	   - The *outline* does SEE the constraint in prose: `reasoning_trace.jsonl:1` (the `_outline` deepseek call) reads *"the question asks to focus on high-quality English-language journal articles… ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"* and resolves to prioritize T1/T2/T3. 23	   - `retrieval/intake_constraint_extractor.py:130-133` — `PG_EXTRACT_USER_CONSTRAINTS` **defaults OFF** and is only set to `1` in `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py:5834`. The section writer (the champion `_call_section` path) is **NOT capped at 2400.** Because of the floor at `:4571-4575`, content is floored to `16384 + 8192 = 24576`. 115	
116	Therefore:
117	
118	- A narrow **post-fetch journal-only mask** once worked and caused evidence collapse.
119	- The intended **prompt-derived, general, fetch/search-stage eligibility system** has never been fully wired on this path.
120	- The current behavior is not a regression from a working smart gate; it is an incomplete replacement for the retired narrow filter.
121	
122	### Correct general fix
123	
124	The fix must be an acquisition contract, not another report-time screen:
125	
126	1. **Compile one typed `AcquisitionContract` from the task.** It should represent source type, language, date interval, geographic/entity scope, publication status, and requested quality semantics as `{operator, strength, normalized value, exact prompt span}`. Deterministic parsing handles explicit clauses; an LLM fallback interprets genuinely opaque constraints. No task numbers, industry lists, journal lists, or fixed descriptive-word lists.
127	
128	2. **Make the contract mandatory on every acquisition route.** The same contract/hash must reach primary retrieval, outline-gap retrieval, FS-researcher, IterResearch, CRAG/deepeners, recovery/refetch, and resume. A caller without it must fail acquisition for a constrained task rather than silently issue unscoped searches.
129	
130	3. **Select backends by required metadata capability.** For a hard journal-article constraint, use scholarly indexes and metadata resolution as the admission path. Generic web search may discover a DOI or canonical record, but the web page itself is not corpus-eligible.
131	
132	4. **Apply metadata eligibility before body fetch.** Definite source-type, language, or date mismatches are rejected before download. Unknowns enter metadata resolution—not the corpus—until publication type, canonical version, language, and date are resolved.
133	
134	5. **Validate at corpus admission.** If body-language inspection or canonical metadata contradicts the pre-fetch record, reject that candidate before corpus commit and immediately backfill it. This is acquisition-stage eligibility, not post-generation content removal.
135	
136	6. **Define quality from prompt semantics plus observable source-independent features.** Use publication status, document type, peer-review evidence where available, retraction/version state, stable bibliographic identity, and task relevance. Do not encode named venues or word lists as proxies.
137	
138	7. **Backfill by prompt-derived coverage facets.** Continue retrieval until qualified evidence covers the task’s requested dimensions. Filtering without replacement repeats the historical 59→11 failure and damages Comprehensiveness.
139	
140	8. **Emit an acquisition receipt.** It must contain the prompt-contract hash, backend filters, candidate decisions and reasons, unresolved count, qualified count per facet, and canonical-version substitutions. Prebuilt compose must require a matching receipt or rebuild the corpus; `not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus` cannot be accepted for a constrained run.
141	
142	9. **Test generality metamorphically.** Paraphrase source, language, and date constraints; test mixed hard/soft requirements and prompts with no source constraint. The invariant is that the task’s own contract controls acquisition, while unconstrained tasks remain broad.
143	
144	### Expected RACE effect
145	
146	The gate directly targets the 0.25 Instruction-Following dimension. Task 72 assigns 0.15 of that dimension to journal-only compliance and 0.10 to English-only compliance ([criteria](/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl:72)). It also affects the Comprehensiveness criterion for depth and representativeness of qualified literature.
147	
148	But there are two important limits:
149	
150	- The RACE cleaner removes citation markers and the entire references section, so the judge cannot directly inspect most source identities. Direct IF gains from cleaner bibliography compliance may therefore be muted.
151	- Filtering alone can reduce Comprehensiveness. Only replacement with broader, stronger qualified evidence can improve both dimensions.
152	
153	My estimate is **0 to +0.005 for the gate alone**, likely sub-noise; **+0.007 to +0.015 when it also replaces weak sources with broad, higher-value journal evidence**. A +0.02 move requires that improved evidence materially changes the synthesis, not merely the bibliography.

157	### Critical correction to the seed finding
158	
159	The configured 2K/16K K3 reasoning caps are mostly **not effective K3 caps**.
160	
161	`OpenRouterClient.generate()` calls the internal request path with `reasoning_enabled=False` ([client generate](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:3357)). The client sends a reasoning configuration only for `_ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS`, explicitly enabled calls, or `_REASONING_FIRST_MODELS` ([request branches](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:2010)). 162	
163	Consequently:
164	
165	- `reasoning_max_tokens=2048` or `16384` supplied by these callers is ignored for K3.
166	- K3 uses the provider’s default always-thinking behavior.
167	- The enclosing `max_tokens` is the effective combined reasoning-plus-visible-output ceiling.
168	- That explains matrix responses with 5,997 reasoning tokens inside a 6,000-token total ceiling.

170	The existing globals `PG_REASONING_FIRST_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=32768` and `PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP=384000` do not protect K3 because it is absent from that registry ([config](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/config_defaults.py:744)). The token-limit resolver’s static model table also omits K3 ([resolver](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/token_limit_resolver.py:129)).

209	
210	### Correct K3 policy
211	
212	Current official metadata identifies K3 as a reasoning model with a 1,048,576-token context window, reasoning enabled by default, supported reasoning effort up to `max`, and pricing of $3/M input and $15/M output; OpenRouter currently reports no published top-provider completion cap rather than an unlimited cap ([OpenRouter model API](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models), [K3 model page](https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k3-20260715)). Moonshot’s own configuration identifies K3 as `always_thinking` and supports `max` reasoning effort ([Moonshot configuration](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-code/en/configuration/config-files.html)).
213	
214	I recommend:
215	
216	- **Client capability:** recognize K3 through provider/model capability metadata, not only a hardcoded slug set. Send `reasoning.effort="max"` explicitly and register all K3 call types in tracing.
217	- **Preflight:** query model and endpoint metadata, then run a completion-cap smoke. Treat a null published cap as unknown, not unlimited.
218	- **Main sections:** request a **64K combined completion ceiling**, effort `max`, with a context-aware bound of `validated_provider_cap` and `context − input − safety margin`. This is insurance; the audited sections did not hit 24,576.
219	- **Synthesis matrix:** use at least **32K**, preferably **64K**, because a prior direct K3 audit used 28,862 completion tokens—23,739 reasoning plus roughly 5,123 visible content ([K3 audit](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/research/audits/deepaudit_kimi.md:1)). Retry from scratch on `finish_reason=length`, increasing to the validated cap. Never silently omit all matrices.
220	- **Compact K3 side calls:** 32K combined with effort `max` if K3 remains the selected model. Better still, make deterministic extraction and rendering handle tasks that do not benefit from long-form reasoning.
221	- **No blanket global-max rewrite:** use per-call-class K3 policy. A universal 384K request would worsen latency/capacity without improving terse tasks.
222	
223	There is no pricing tier cliff. The real cliff is architectural: when K3’s reasoning fills the shared completion ceiling, visible content drops to zero. Reasoning is billed as output at $15/M. Fully consuming 64K costs approximately $0.96 per call, or $9.60 for ten matrix calls; actual usage, not the cap, is billed. Latency and the current single-provider/capacity situation are the practical downsides. The audited sections already took roughly 276–440 seconds each ([first](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4672), [last](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4748)).
224	
225	**Magnitude:** fixing matrix truncation is necessary. Raising the main cap alone is likely **0 to +0.003**, sub-noise. Combining correct K3 handling with focused high-value evidence and an explicit synthesis prompt is plausibly **+0.010 to +0.020**.

284	Mechanical raw-versus-cleaned comparisons confirm that:
285	
286	- GFM pipe tables survive;
287	- bullets survive;
288	- bold markers survive;
289	- ordinary headings survive;
290	- citations and the References heading/body disappear.
291	
292	Therefore textual tables, bullets, and bold are worth testing. A PNG/image-only chart is effectively invisible to this judge. A chart would need an accompanying textual table or data summary, making the image itself nearly zero-EV.
293	
294	### Correct deterministic artifact design
295	
296	1. Introduce a common `ReportBlock` representation: `paragraph`, `subheading`, `bullet_list`, and `gfm_table`. Carry blocks through section writing, resolution, assembly, and cleaner canaries instead of flattening everything into strings.
297	
298	2. Build comparison facts before generation from the qualified evidence substrate using a general schema such as construct, value, unit, context/population, time horizon, method/design, boundary condition, and source ID.
299	
300	3. Render comparison tables deterministically from comparable fact groups. Do not ask K3 to invent Markdown and then silently discard invalid rows. Non-comparable findings remain in prose; no generated content is removed.
301	
302	4. Wire artifact blocks into the audited/champion compose assembly. A `MultiSectionResult` table or block that is not inserted into `report.md` is not a fired feature.
303	
304	5. Reconcile the prompts: when structured output is active, remove the contradictory final “write one paragraph” instruction. Preserve section-native Markdown regardless of whether the simpler paragraph-spacing lever is active.
305	
306	6. Add a neutral, deterministic Key Findings block by copying selected body sentences. Remove the false “strict_verify-passed” label when strict verification is off ([current label](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py:975)).
307	
308	7. Use bullets only for parallel enumerable findings and bold only for short labels. Dense literature synthesis should remain prose plus selective tables, not become a slide deck.
309	
310	8. Require behavioral output assertions: if comparable cross-study data exists, `report.md` and the cleaned RACE article must contain a valid table with nonzero rows; if a parallel findings block is requested, bullet and bold counts must be nonzero. No “call completed” or “flag enabled” surrogate.
311	
312	9. Defer image charts until a judge-path experiment proves image visibility. Under the current text-only scorer, prioritize tables.
313	
314	## Complete forward plan, ranked by expected value
315	
316	| Rank | Work package | Acceptance test | Expected RACE delta |
317	|---:|---|---|---:|
318	| 1 | **Qualified evidence replacement + denser K3 synthesis** | Prompt-derived facets receive broad qualified evidence; sections explicitly compare agreement, disagreement, methods, contexts, mechanisms, and boundary conditions | **+0.015 to +0.030 combined**; only credible standalone +0.02-class package |
319	| 2 | **End-to-end acquisition contract with qualified backfill** | Every acquisition path presents a matching receipt; no hard-mismatch candidate enters the corpus; coverage does not fall | Gate alone 0 to +0.005; with replacement **+0.007 to +0.015** |
320	| 3 | **Deterministic cross-study tables and `ReportBlock` wiring** | Comparable evidence produces visible, valid tables in raw and cleaned articles; no silent zero-table outcome | **+0.004 to +0.012** |
321	| 4 | **Correct K3 capability and budgets** | Explicit effort=max; no `finish_reason=length`; matrix visible; content/reasoning usage traced for every call | Budget alone 0 to +0.003; matrix recovery roughly **+0.002 to +0.008** |
322	| 5 | **Paragraphing, selective bullets, bold labels** | No giant single-paragraph sections; Key Findings visible; clean article retains formatting | **+0.002 to +0.006**, sub-noise alone |
323	| 6 | **Charts/images** | Only pursue if scorer demonstrably consumes pixels | **0 to +0.002** |
324	| 7 | **Reference/citation cosmetic cleanup** | N/A—the cleaner removes them | Approximately **0** to RACE |
325	
326	These ranges are hypotheses, not additive guarantees.
327	
328	### Implementation sequence
329	
330	1. **Create the behavioral baseline.**
331	   - Freeze the audited corpus and cleaned article.
332	   - Record qualified-source share, source/language/date unknown rates, prompt-facet coverage, K3 finish reasons, reasoning/content tokens, visible artifact counts, and all four RACE dimensions.
333	   - Preserve separate same-corpus and fresh-retrieval test arms.
334	
335	2. **Compile and bind the acquisition contract.**
336	   - Implement the typed prompt-derived contract once.
337	   - Thread it through outline-gap, primary, FS, IterResearch, CRAG/deepener, recovery, and resume.
338	   - Add backend capability routing and pre-fetch metadata eligibility.
339	   - Add canonical-version recovery, corpus-admission validation, backfill, and receipts.
340	   - Require receipt compatibility when composing a prebuilt corpus.
341	
342	3. **Build a genuinely higher-value evidence substrate.**
343	   - Retain the entire qualified corpus, but create per-section packs by marginal prompt-facet coverage rather than dumping 559K input tokens into one call.
344	   - Prefer evidence that exposes method, design, context, measure/unit, magnitude, uncertainty, and boundary conditions.
345	   - Ensure requested dimensions—not a fixed task-specific list—receive multiple independent qualified sources.
346	   - Backfill weak facets before generation.
347	
348	4. **Fix K3 as a reasoning-first capability.**
349	   - Send explicit effort `max`.
350	   - Add K3 to capability-aware token and response handling.
351	   - Use 64K section and matrix ceilings within live provider limits.
352	   - Raise compact K3 tasks to 32K where retained.
353	   - Add `synthesis_matrix` and every other actual call type to reasoning traces.
354	   - Retry truncations rather than accepting zero-content output.
355	
356	5. **Rewrite the synthesis task, not just its budget.**
357	   - Remove “write one paragraph.”
358	   - Ask each section to integrate evidence around prompt-derived mechanisms and comparisons.
359	   - Require explicit cross-source convergence, disagreement, methodological reasons for divergence, context/industry boundaries, and implications where supported by the evidence pack.
360	   - Give K3 structured evidence cards rather than a maximally long undifferentiated dump.
361	
362	6. **Replace the current matrix with construction-by-validity.**
363	   - Group pre-generation facts by comparable construct/unit/context.
364	   - Render tables deterministically from those groups.
365	   - Use K3 only to choose an explanatory framing or prose synthesis, not to manufacture table syntax or facts.
366	   - Insert table blocks into the champion compose result.
367	
368	7. **Wire the rest of the presentation layer.**
369	   - Preserve paragraphs and GFM blocks through assembly.
370	   - Insert extractive Key Findings with a truthful neutral label.
371	   - Use selective bullets and bold field labels.
372	   - Run table normalization as repair of syntax, not as an artifact producer.
373	   - Confirm the cleaned article retains every intended block.
374	
375	8. **Replay and isolate causality.**
376	   - Arm A: current output.
377	   - Arm B: same corpus, K3/prompt changes only.
378	   - Arm C: same prose, artifact rendering only.
379	   - Arm D: fresh scoped retrieval with old generation.
380	   - Arm E: fresh scoped retrieval plus dense generation and artifacts.
381	   - This distinguishes bibliography compliance from evidence-quality, generation, and presentation effects.
382	
383	9. **Test generality across the benchmark distribution.**
384	   - Include paraphrased hard/soft source constraints, language/date constraints, mixed constraints, and unconstrained prompts.
385	   - Assert no over-filtering on prompts that allow broad sources.
386	   - Assert the compiled contract—not a task identifier or fixed venue/domain vocabulary—explains every acquisition decision.
387	
388	10. **Use a noise-aware promotion rule.**
389	    - Run paired multi-draw RACE evaluations.
390	    - Treat ≤0.007 as noise.
391	    - Treat +0.008 to +0.014 as promising but needing replication.
392	    - Call a lever a real mover only when its replicated mean exceeds roughly +0.014 or its uncertainty excludes +0.007.
393	    - The combined evidence-plus-generation-plus-table package is the only package I would presently target for a credible **+0.02** result.
394	
395	The strategic conclusion is that the run did not fail because one flag was missing. It combined an unbound acquisition constraint, an unproductive 6K K3 matrix call, an overly restrictive matrix validator, and an assembly path that ignored nearly every artifact producer. The highest-EV correction is to make the prompt govern acquisition, replace weak evidence without losing breadth, give K3 focused high-value material and a real completion budget, and render comparisons by construction.
396

# Independent verdict

Read-only audit complete; I made no repository changes.

The run is genuinely flat. Its three overall scores were 0.5112, 0.5072, and 0.5073, for a 0.50857 mean versus the 0.5084 baseline: **+0.00017**, roughly forty times smaller than the stated ±0.007 judge noise ([R1](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.race_r1.log:16), [R2](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.race_r2.log:16), [R3](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.race_r3.log:17), [baseline](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/docs/race_4dim_action_plan.md:3)). Approximate dimension changes were:

| Dimension | Baseline | All-levers mean | Change |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Comprehensiveness | 0.521 | 0.5197 | −0.0013 |
| Insight | 0.524 | 0.5235 | −0.0005 |
| Instruction following | 0.499 | 0.4996 | +0.0006 |
| Readability | 0.457 | 0.4629 | +0.0059 |

Only Readability moved perceptibly, and even that remained below noise.

The three central findings are:

1. **The smart source/scope gate did not function end to end.** Prompt understanding exists, but no hard source-type/language eligibility contract reaches all search/fetch paths. The audited acquisition applied a topic-relevance demotion after fetching, then the compose path explicitly bypassed scope evaluation.
2. **K3 was catastrophically underbudgeted for the synthesis matrix, but the main section writer was not observed truncating.** More subtly, the nominal 2K/16K K3 reasoning caps were not transmitted at all because K3 is absent from the client’s reasoning-first registry.
3. **No judge-visible artifact rendered.** `chart_alt_chrome: fired=1` is a suppression detector, not chart generation. Tables failed or were unwired; bullet/bold emitters were bypassed; the final report was ten enormous flat sections.

## Area 1 — source/scope gate

### End-to-end finding

The actual path was:

```text
Task prompt
  → constraint-extraction capability recognizes journal/English requirements
  → retrieval projection adds scholarly-looking queries/routes
  ╳ hard typed eligibility policy is not propagated through every caller
  → Serper + Semantic Scholar + additive OpenAlex candidates
  → body fetch occurs
  → topic/content relevance demotes but keeps
  → source-type classification occurs after fetch
  ╳ source-quality/RQ eligibility not called by audited compose
  → prebuilt corpus loaded directly
  → all retained section prose + all bibliography rows assembled
  → non-journal sources reach report
```

### 1. Constraint understanding exists, but the seed overstates what was run

Task 72 unambiguously says to cite only high-quality, English-language journal articles ([task prompt](/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/prompt_data/query_task72.jsonl:1)). The generic standalone extractor has journal aliases and language handling ([source aliases](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py:69), [languages](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py:97)), while the newer intake adapter can deterministically compile the exact prompt into a hard `peer_reviewed_journal` restriction ([test](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/tests/planning/test_candidate_adapter.py:62), [merge logic](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/candidate_adapter.py:745)).

The run’s outline also understood the requirement semantically: its first checklist names the gap as evidence “limited to high-quality, English-language journal articles” ([gen.log:17](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:17)).

But that does **not** prove the typed intake contract was bound to this acquisition. The standalone LLM extractor is explicitly a standalone component, its live call defaults off, and the run emits no acquisition-contract receipt showing that a hard journal/language constraint reached retrieval ([extractor declaration](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py:3), [live default](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/instruction/constraint_extractor.py:364)).

**Verdict:** extraction capability is not the primary defect. Binding and enforcement are.

### 2. Query generation biases; it does not exclude

`retrieval_projection.py` maps journal-like requirements toward `primary_literature`, but describes the projection as additive routing and query amplification rather than a mandatory eligibility filter ([policy model](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py:1), [source mapping](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py:82), [query amplification](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py:488)). Hard source restrictions are default-off/degradable, and conversion to the older retrieval protocol can downgrade allowed source kinds to a preference ([hard restriction](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py:62), [downgrade](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py:329)).

FS-researcher can prepend constraint-oriented queries, but is default-off and passes a `retrieval_policy` into a wrapper that accepts arbitrary keyword arguments and then does not forward the policy to the live retriever ([FS default](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/fs_researcher_query_gen.py:44), [query construction](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/fs_researcher_query_gen.py:970), [discarding wrapper](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:10800)). IterResearch has no equivalent typed policy parameter ([IterResearch interface](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/iterresearch_query_gen.py:57)).

The audited retrieval was driven by the outline gap tool. That call forwards frame/protocol information but no typed `retrieval_policy` ([gap retrieval call](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:799)). Thus “primary literature” could influence which backends were added, but nothing required every returned item to be a journal article.

### 3. Search and fetch do not enforce source type

The live retriever accepts `retrieval_policy`, but its concrete policy application is essentially an OpenAlex date window ([signature](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:5666), [date application](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:5942)). It then runs:

- Serper generally ([live_retriever.py:6072](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:6072));
- Semantic Scholar generally ([live_retriever.py:6091](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:6091));
- OpenAlex additively ([live_retriever.py:6118](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:6118)).

Actual run evidence matches that path: Serper and Semantic Scholar returned 29 candidates ([gen.log:25](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:25)); the TechJournal blog was queued ([gen.log:39](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:39)) and successfully fetched by Trafilatura with 7,871 characters ([gen.log:65](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:65)).

Document/source-type classification occurs only after body retrieval ([live_retriever.py:7287](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:7287)). There is no pre-fetch test that says “the prompt requires journal articles; this candidate is a blog; do not fetch or admit it.”

### 4. The gate that fired was topic relevance, and it kept failures

The live retriever ranks candidates for relevance and explicitly implements a demote-not-drop path ([selection](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:6455), [demotion](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:6495)). The log is conclusive:

- “fetched all 29; demote-not-drop” ([gen.log:35](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:35));
- 29 successful fetches ([gen.log:1185](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:1185));
- content-relevance demotion with no deletion ([gen.log:1189](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:1189));
- topic gate “DEMOTED(kept, disclosed) 23” and deleted zero ([gen.log:1230](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:1230)).

That is a topicality control, not a source-eligibility control.

### 5. The nominal source-quality gates are post-fetch and bypassed

`rq_eligibility.py` describes itself as a citable-generation eligibility demotion layer, defaults off, and treats unknown metadata as fail-open ([module contract](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py:1), [defaults](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py:76), [unknown language/date](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py:486)). Even known mismatches receive `demoted_ineligible_kept`, not exclusion ([rq_eligibility.py:511](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/rq_eligibility.py:511)). Its only substantive consumer is the post-retrieval evidence selector, where it changes sorting weights rather than membership ([evidence_selector.py:3060](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py:3060), [weighted sort](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/evidence_selector.py:3229)).

`quality_eligibility.py` is also post-fetch ([module contract](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py:1)). Its source-kind mask arms only when a hard flag, force flag, adequacy threshold, and matching acquisition receipt all exist ([mask condition](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py:674)); otherwise it degrades to preference and disclosure ([degradation](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py:701)). Production currently reads a future/local `_acquisition_receipt`, but no production assignment exists on the traced path ([runner seam](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14760)).

Its tier bands and named-host rules also are not the general prompt-digesting design requested here ([quality bands](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py:132), [host patterns](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py:139)).

### 6. Compose explicitly bypasses retrieval/scope enforcement

The audited driver identifies itself as composition from a prebuilt corpus “minus retrieval/scope machinery” ([driver header](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:2)). It directly loads the corpus ([compose driver:260](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:260)), passes it to the multi-section generator ([compose driver:374](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:374)), assembles the kept section strings ([compose driver:443](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:443)), and writes every bibliography row ([compose driver:471](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:471)).

The resulting summary explicitly reports `retrieval_scope_status="not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus"` ([compose_summary.json:39](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/compose_summary.json:39)). This is not merely missing telemetry: the relevant selector/gates were not in the compose path.

The consequences are visible:

- The prose claims to be “privileging high-quality English-language journal articles” ([report.md:9](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:9)).
- The references include a Bipartisan Policy Center issue brief, SSRN, OECD webpages/reports, Wikipedia, MIT PubPub, an SHRM report, and a CaixaBank blog ([report.md:119](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:119), [Wikipedia](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:122), [MIT](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:125), [SHRM](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:129), [CaixaBank](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:134)).
- Non-journal material influenced body claims, not merely an unused bibliography; for example OECD and SSRN projections appear directly in the opening synthesis ([report.md:7](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:7), [report.md:11](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:11)).

### 7. Did journal filtering ever work?

**Yes, but not the intended smart fetch-time design.**

Git history shows:

- `2b42bc5f` on 2026-07-07 introduced the old journal-only hard filter;
- `b67506a3` on 2026-07-17 generalized/retired it;
- `3d891790` on 2026-07-21 added the newer evidence-substrate eligibility levers.

The current `journal_only_filter.py` is deliberately retired and returns inactive/identity behavior ([retirement](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/nodes/journal_only_filter.py:76), [identity filter](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/nodes/journal_only_filter.py:293)). Historical logs show it reducing 59 items to 11, excluding 48, then aborting because only five journal sources remained against a twelve-source adequacy floor ([session_log.md:5144](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/logs/session_log.md:5144)). The ensuing review rejected that tunnel-vision/starvation behavior ([session_log.md:5155](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/logs/session_log.md:5155)). A prior audit nevertheless confirms that the old mask really removed 15 of 23 non-journal sources ([journal audit](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/audits/I-ready-017/journal_only_s11_audit.md:14)).

Therefore:

- A narrow **post-fetch journal-only mask** once worked and caused evidence collapse.
- The intended **prompt-derived, general, fetch/search-stage eligibility system** has never been fully wired on this path.
- The current behavior is not a regression from a working smart gate; it is an incomplete replacement for the retired narrow filter.

### Correct general fix

The fix must be an acquisition contract, not another report-time screen:

1. **Compile one typed `AcquisitionContract` from the task.** It should represent source type, language, date interval, geographic/entity scope, publication status, and requested quality semantics as `{operator, strength, normalized value, exact prompt span}`. Deterministic parsing handles explicit clauses; an LLM fallback interprets genuinely opaque constraints. No task numbers, industry lists, journal lists, or fixed descriptive-word lists.

2. **Make the contract mandatory on every acquisition route.** The same contract/hash must reach primary retrieval, outline-gap retrieval, FS-researcher, IterResearch, CRAG/deepeners, recovery/refetch, and resume. A caller without it must fail acquisition for a constrained task rather than silently issue unscoped searches.

3. **Select backends by required metadata capability.** For a hard journal-article constraint, use scholarly indexes and metadata resolution as the admission path. Generic web search may discover a DOI or canonical record, but the web page itself is not corpus-eligible.

4. **Apply metadata eligibility before body fetch.** Definite source-type, language, or date mismatches are rejected before download. Unknowns enter metadata resolution—not the corpus—until publication type, canonical version, language, and date are resolved.

5. **Validate at corpus admission.** If body-language inspection or canonical metadata contradicts the pre-fetch record, reject that candidate before corpus commit and immediately backfill it. This is acquisition-stage eligibility, not post-generation content removal.

6. **Define quality from prompt semantics plus observable source-independent features.** Use publication status, document type, peer-review evidence where available, retraction/version state, stable bibliographic identity, and task relevance. Do not encode named venues or word lists as proxies.

7. **Backfill by prompt-derived coverage facets.** Continue retrieval until qualified evidence covers the task’s requested dimensions. Filtering without replacement repeats the historical 59→11 failure and damages Comprehensiveness.

8. **Emit an acquisition receipt.** It must contain the prompt-contract hash, backend filters, candidate decisions and reasons, unresolved count, qualified count per facet, and canonical-version substitutions. Prebuilt compose must require a matching receipt or rebuild the corpus; `not_evaluated_prebuilt_corpus` cannot be accepted for a constrained run.

9. **Test generality metamorphically.** Paraphrase source, language, and date constraints; test mixed hard/soft requirements and prompts with no source constraint. The invariant is that the task’s own contract controls acquisition, while unconstrained tasks remain broad.

### Expected RACE effect

The gate directly targets the 0.25 Instruction-Following dimension. Task 72 assigns 0.15 of that dimension to journal-only compliance and 0.10 to English-only compliance ([criteria](/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl:72)). It also affects the Comprehensiveness criterion for depth and representativeness of qualified literature.

But there are two important limits:

- The RACE cleaner removes citation markers and the entire references section, so the judge cannot directly inspect most source identities. Direct IF gains from cleaner bibliography compliance may therefore be muted.
- Filtering alone can reduce Comprehensiveness. Only replacement with broader, stronger qualified evidence can improve both dimensions.

My estimate is **0 to +0.005 for the gate alone**, likely sub-noise; **+0.007 to +0.015 when it also replaces weak sources with broad, higher-value journal evidence**. A +0.02 move requires that improved evidence materially changes the synthesis, not merely the bibliography.

## Area 2 — K3 reasoning and completion budgets

### Critical correction to the seed finding

The configured 2K/16K K3 reasoning caps are mostly **not effective K3 caps**.

`OpenRouterClient.generate()` calls the internal request path with `reasoning_enabled=False` ([client generate](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:3357)). The client sends a reasoning configuration only for `_ALWAYS_REASON_MODELS`, explicitly enabled calls, or `_REASONING_FIRST_MODELS` ([request branches](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:2010)). That registry contains GLM and DeepSeek models but **not** `moonshotai/kimi-k3` ([registry](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/openrouter_client.py:821)).

Consequently:

- `reasoning_max_tokens=2048` or `16384` supplied by these callers is ignored for K3.
- K3 uses the provider’s default always-thinking behavior.
- The enclosing `max_tokens` is the effective combined reasoning-plus-visible-output ceiling.
- That explains matrix responses with 5,997 reasoning tokens inside a 6,000-token total ceiling.

The existing globals `PG_REASONING_FIRST_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=32768` and `PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP=384000` do not protect K3 because it is absent from that registry ([config](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/config_defaults.py:744)). The token-limit resolver’s static model table also omits K3 ([resolver](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/llm/token_limit_resolver.py:129)).

### Call-by-call audit

| Call class | Configured intent | Effective audited behavior | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| Outline | Script total 131,072; reasoning 32,768 ([script](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/scripts/run_raw_a.sh:45)) | Used DeepSeek, not K3; completed with 9,071 output tokens ([log](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:11)) | Not a K3 problem |

### Matrix failure was total

Ten matrix calls were made:

- Six hit exactly 6,000 output tokens, with 5,997 reasoning tokens and zero visible content; representative failures are at [4685–4689](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4685), [4692–4696](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4692), [4721–4725](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4721), [4738–4742](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4738), [4751–4755](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4751), and [4757–4761](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4757).
- Their reasoning outputs were 23,599–26,631 characters and `finish_reason='length'`.
- The other four returned only 647, 685, 733, and 21 content characters, then were suppressed ([first](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4680), [second](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4705), [third](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4713), [fourth](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4728)).
- Every matrix trace record was also lost because `synthesis_matrix` is absent from the reasoning-trace call-type allowlist ([log](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4681)).

Budget is only half the matrix defect. The parser requires a canonical six-cell row, a citation-only reference cell, no citations in content cells, and each substantive cell to be a verbatim span attributable to one cited clause ([validator](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:8186)). That specification is hostile to real cross-study synthesis. Even completed tables were rejected.

The correct response is not another silent suppression layer. Construct rows from typed pre-generation facts so they are valid by construction, retry a truncated artifact call, and fail the artifact acceptance test loudly if it still produces no table.

### Main sections were not demonstrably budget-starved

The ten main K3 section calls produced 6,986–14,632 total output tokens, with 5,931–11,331 reasoning tokens, all below the effective 24,576 ceiling. Representative completed calls are [4671–4672](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4671), [4702–4703](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4702), and [4744–4745](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4744).

The largest call consumed 559,536 input tokens, emitted 14,632 total output tokens, took 370.5 seconds, and cost $1.8981 ([gen.log:4745](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4745)). That is evidence of an unfocused evidence pack, not an output ceiling collision. Merely raising the cap does not force denser synthesis.

The final user instruction also says “Write the … paragraph” when block rendering is off ([multi_section_generator.py:4522](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:4522)). The model was therefore being asked for flat prose, not maximally structured cross-study analysis.

### Correct K3 policy

Current official metadata identifies K3 as a reasoning model with a 1,048,576-token context window, reasoning enabled by default, supported reasoning effort up to `max`, and pricing of $3/M input and $15/M output; OpenRouter currently reports no published top-provider completion cap rather than an unlimited cap ([OpenRouter model API](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models), [K3 model page](https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k3-20260715)). Moonshot’s own configuration identifies K3 as `always_thinking` and supports `max` reasoning effort ([Moonshot configuration](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-code/en/configuration/config-files.html)).

I recommend:

- **Client capability:** recognize K3 through provider/model capability metadata, not only a hardcoded slug set. Send `reasoning.effort="max"` explicitly and register all K3 call types in tracing.
- **Preflight:** query model and endpoint metadata, then run a completion-cap smoke. Treat a null published cap as unknown, not unlimited.
- **Main sections:** request a **64K combined completion ceiling**, effort `max`, with a context-aware bound of `validated_provider_cap` and `context − input − safety margin`. This is insurance; the audited sections did not hit 24,576.
- **Synthesis matrix:** use at least **32K**, preferably **64K**, because a prior direct K3 audit used 28,862 completion tokens—23,739 reasoning plus roughly 5,123 visible content ([K3 audit](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/research/audits/deepaudit_kimi.md:1)). Retry from scratch on `finish_reason=length`, increasing to the validated cap. Never silently omit all matrices.
- **Compact K3 side calls:** 32K combined with effort `max` if K3 remains the selected model. Better still, make deterministic extraction and rendering handle tasks that do not benefit from long-form reasoning.
- **No blanket global-max rewrite:** use per-call-class K3 policy. A universal 384K request would worsen latency/capacity without improving terse tasks.

There is no pricing tier cliff. The real cliff is architectural: when K3’s reasoning fills the shared completion ceiling, visible content drops to zero. Reasoning is billed as output at $15/M. Fully consuming 64K costs approximately $0.96 per call, or $9.60 for ten matrix calls; actual usage, not the cap, is billed. Latency and the current single-provider/capacity situation are the practical downsides. The audited sections already took roughly 276–440 seconds each ([first](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4672), [last](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4748)).

**Magnitude:** fixing matrix truncation is necessary. Raising the main cap alone is likely **0 to +0.003**, sub-noise. Combining correct K3 handling with focused high-value evidence and an explicit synthesis prompt is plausibly **+0.010 to +0.020**.

## Area 3 — artifact inventory

The complete report is 288 lines and 13,151 words with 171 bibliography entries ([report start](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/report.md:1), [compose summary](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run/compose_summary.json:19)). A full Markdown scan found:

- zero pipe-table rows;
- zero bullet/list lines;
- zero `**bold**` markers;
- zero image/chart Markdown;
- only headings and very long paragraphs.

Several current relation paths use banned entailment/NLI and must not be revived |

Two additional adversarial findings:

- `summary_table.py` claims task 72 explicitly requests named table columns ([module header](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/summary_table.py:3)); the actual task prompt contains no table request. That documentation is stale. The module also relies on curated field vocabularies, so it is not the general prompt-digesting solution requested here.
- Internal outline bullets, evidence-menu lists, JSON slot fields, and table-aware retrieval chunks are not report artifacts. Counting them would repeat the “machinery exists” mistake.

### The alleged chart path is not chart generation

`chart_alt_chrome` is explicitly a regex that recognizes screen-reader axis narration and returns `True` to suppress it ([definition](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/weighted_enrichment.py:1871), [activation](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/weighted_enrichment.py:3053)). Therefore [gen.log:4593](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/outputs/all_levers_run.gen.log:4593) proves that chart-like scraped text was detected, not that a chart was created.

Actual chart renderers live outside the requested directories in legacy paths:

- Matplotlib/chart Markdown in [`agents/synthesizer.py`](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/agents/synthesizer.py:564);
- base64 PNG output in [`nodes/synthesize.py`](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/nodes/synthesize.py:148);
- an explicitly unwired visual tool in [`visual_generator.py`](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/tools/visual_generator.py:6).

None is called by the champion compose path.

### What the RACE cleaner preserves

The cleaner removes citation markers, footnotes, and reference-only material, then retains the other article content ([cleaner](/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/clean_prompt.py:28)). The scorer interpolates the cleaned article as text; it does not render or inspect images ([scorer](/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/third_party/deep_research_bench/deepresearch_bench_race.py:84)).

Mechanical raw-versus-cleaned comparisons confirm that:

- GFM pipe tables survive;
- bullets survive;
- bold markers survive;
- ordinary headings survive;
- citations and the References heading/body disappear.

Therefore textual tables, bullets, and bold are worth testing. A PNG/image-only chart is effectively invisible to this judge. A chart would need an accompanying textual table or data summary, making the image itself nearly zero-EV.

### Correct deterministic artifact design

1. Introduce a common `ReportBlock` representation: `paragraph`, `subheading`, `bullet_list`, and `gfm_table`. Carry blocks through section writing, resolution, assembly, and cleaner canaries instead of flattening everything into strings.

2. Build comparison facts before generation from the qualified evidence substrate using a general schema such as construct, value, unit, context/population, time horizon, method/design, boundary condition, and source ID.

3. Render comparison tables deterministically from comparable fact groups. Do not ask K3 to invent Markdown and then silently discard invalid rows. Non-comparable findings remain in prose; no generated content is removed.

4. Wire artifact blocks into the audited/champion compose assembly. A `MultiSectionResult` table or block that is not inserted into `report.md` is not a fired feature.

5. Reconcile the prompts: when structured output is active, remove the contradictory final “write one paragraph” instruction. Preserve section-native Markdown regardless of whether the simpler paragraph-spacing lever is active.

6. Add a neutral, deterministic Key Findings block by copying selected body sentences. Remove the false “strict_verify-passed” label when strict verification is off ([current label](/home/polaris/wt/faithoff/src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py:975)).

7. Use bullets only for parallel enumerable findings and bold only for short labels. Dense literature synthesis should remain prose plus selective tables, not become a slide deck.

8. Require behavioral output assertions: if comparable cross-study data exists, `report.md` and the cleaned RACE article must contain a valid table with nonzero rows; if a parallel findings block is requested, bullet and bold counts must be nonzero. No “call completed” or “flag enabled” surrogate.

9. Defer image charts until a judge-path experiment proves image visibility. Under the current text-only scorer, prioritize tables.

## Complete forward plan, ranked by expected value

| Rank | Work package | Acceptance test | Expected RACE delta |
|---:|---|---|---:|
| 1 | **Qualified evidence replacement + denser K3 synthesis** | Prompt-derived facets receive broad qualified evidence; sections explicitly compare agreement, disagreement, methods, contexts, mechanisms, and boundary conditions | **+0.015 to +0.030 combined**; only credible standalone +0.02-class package |
| 2 | **End-to-end acquisition contract with qualified backfill** | Every acquisition path presents a matching receipt; no hard-mismatch candidate enters the corpus; coverage does not fall | Gate alone 0 to +0.005; with replacement **+0.007 to +0.015** |
| 3 | **Deterministic cross-study tables and `ReportBlock` wiring** | Comparable evidence produces visible, valid tables in raw and cleaned articles; no silent zero-table outcome | **+0.004 to +0.012** |
| 4 | **Correct K3 capability and budgets** | Explicit effort=max; no `finish_reason=length`; matrix visible; content/reasoning usage traced for every call | Budget alone 0 to +0.003; matrix recovery roughly **+0.002 to +0.008** |
| 5 | **Paragraphing, selective bullets, bold labels** | No giant single-paragraph sections; Key Findings visible; clean article retains formatting | **+0.002 to +0.006**, sub-noise alone |
| 6 | **Charts/images** | Only pursue if scorer demonstrably consumes pixels | **0 to +0.002** |
| 7 | **Reference/citation cosmetic cleanup** | N/A—the cleaner removes them | Approximately **0** to RACE |

These ranges are hypotheses, not additive guarantees.

### Implementation sequence

1. **Create the behavioral baseline.**
   - Freeze the audited corpus and cleaned article.
   - Record qualified-source share, source/language/date unknown rates, prompt-facet coverage, K3 finish reasons, reasoning/content tokens, visible artifact counts, and all four RACE dimensions.
   - Preserve separate same-corpus and fresh-retrieval test arms.

2. **Compile and bind the acquisition contract.**
   - Implement the typed prompt-derived contract once.
   - Thread it through outline-gap, primary, FS, IterResearch, CRAG/deepener, recovery, and resume.
   - Add backend capability routing and pre-fetch metadata eligibility.
   - Add canonical-version recovery, corpus-admission validation, backfill, and receipts.
   - Require receipt compatibility when composing a prebuilt corpus.

3. **Build a genuinely higher-value evidence substrate.**
   - Retain the entire qualified corpus, but create per-section packs by marginal prompt-facet coverage rather than dumping 559K input tokens into one call.
   - Prefer evidence that exposes method, design, context, measure/unit, magnitude, uncertainty, and boundary conditions.
   - Ensure requested dimensions—not a fixed task-specific list—receive multiple independent qualified sources.
   - Backfill weak facets before generation.

4. **Fix K3 as a reasoning-first capability.**
   - Send explicit effort `max`.
   - Add K3 to capability-aware token and response handling.
   - Use 64K section and matrix ceilings within live provider limits.
   - Raise compact K3 tasks to 32K where retained.
   - Add `synthesis_matrix` and every other actual call type to reasoning traces.
   - Retry truncations rather than accepting zero-content output.

5. **Rewrite the synthesis task, not just its budget.**
   - Remove “write one paragraph.”
   - Ask each section to integrate evidence around prompt-derived mechanisms and comparisons.
   - Require explicit cross-source convergence, disagreement, methodological reasons for divergence, context/industry boundaries, and implications where supported by the evidence pack.
   - Give K3 structured evidence cards rather than a maximally long undifferentiated dump.

6. **Replace the current matrix with construction-by-validity.**
   - Group pre-generation facts by comparable construct/unit/context.
   - Render tables deterministically from those groups.
   - Use K3 only to choose an explanatory framing or prose synthesis, not to manufacture table syntax or facts.
   - Insert table blocks into the champion compose result.

7. **Wire the rest of the presentation layer.**
   - Preserve paragraphs and GFM blocks through assembly.
   - Insert extractive Key Findings with a truthful neutral label.
   - Use selective bullets and bold field labels.
   - Run table normalization as repair of syntax, not as an artifact producer.
   - Confirm the cleaned article retains every intended block.

8. **Replay and isolate causality.**
   - Arm A: current output.
   - Arm B: same corpus, K3/prompt changes only.
   - Arm C: same prose, artifact rendering only.
   - Arm D: fresh scoped retrieval with old generation.
   - Arm E: fresh scoped retrieval plus dense generation and artifacts.
   - This distinguishes bibliography compliance from evidence-quality, generation, and presentation effects.

9. **Test generality across the benchmark distribution.**
   - Include paraphrased hard/soft source constraints, language/date constraints, mixed constraints, and unconstrained prompts.
   - Assert no over-filtering on prompts that allow broad sources.
   - Assert the compiled contract—not a task identifier or fixed venue/domain vocabulary—explains every acquisition decision.

10. **Use a noise-aware promotion rule.**
    - Run paired multi-draw RACE evaluations.
    - Treat ≤0.007 as noise.
    - Treat +0.008 to +0.014 as promising but needing replication.
    - Call a lever a real mover only when its replicated mean exceeds roughly +0.014 or its uncertainty excludes +0.007.
    - The combined evidence-plus-generation-plus-table package is the only package I would presently target for a credible **+0.02** result.

The strategic conclusion is that the run did not fail because one flag was missing. It combined an unbound acquisition constraint, an unproductive 6K K3 matrix call, an overly restrictive matrix validator, and an assembly path that ignored nearly every artifact producer. The highest-EV correction is to make the prompt govern acquisition, replace weak evidence without losing breadth, give K3 focused high-value material and a real completion budget, and render comparisons by construction.

"""Deterministic, verified-only SUMMARY TABLE renderer (I-deepfix-001 P7, #1344).

Some research prompts explicitly ask the report to end with a summary TABLE and name
the exact column headers, e.g. drb_72:

    "... please create a summary table ... The table column headers should be
    "Research Literature", "Country/Region", "Application Area/Occupation",
    "Specific Applications and Impacts", and "Key Risks and Limitations"."

The multi-section generator produces span-verified narrative prose but does not emit
this requested GFM table, so ``report.md`` renders zero table rows and the DRB-II
"presentation" rubric fails on "article does not contain a summary table".

This module renders that table WITHOUT any LLM call, purely from ALREADY-verified
content, so it introduces NO new claim (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a summary table is a
presentation of already-verified findings). Faithfulness contract:

* Every row is one CITED source that carries at least one strict-verify-passed claim.
* The "Research Literature" cell is bibliographic metadata (author / title / ``[N]``)
  drawn from the numbered bibliography — an identity, not a claim. Its source_title and
  authors are CHROME-SCREENED with the same predicate as the claim cell (a chrome /
  interstitial title or license/boilerplate author is dropped, the label falling back to a
  clean citation form) so no page furniture renders in the cell; the claim/row is kept.
* The "Specific Applications and Impacts" (claim-role) cell is the source's own
  verified sentence, verbatim (whitespace-normalised, excerpted with an ellipsis),
  carrying its ``[N]`` citation.
* The Country/Region, Application Area/Occupation and Key Risks cells surface ONLY
  short terms that appear VERBATIM in that source's verified spans (high-precision
  curated vocabularies). A cell with no verbatim match renders an em dash ``—`` — an
  honest DISCLOSED GAP; nothing is inferred or invented.

The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding)
is UNTOUCHED — this reads the finished, verified bibliography + kept-sentence objects
and emits markdown. Behaviour is gated behind the default-ON kill-switch
``PG_RENDER_SUMMARY_TABLE`` (LAW VI); OFF => no table => byte-identical report.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# --- canary / marker --------------------------------------------------------
CANARY_TAG = "[summary_table]"
# Distinctive idempotency marker embedded once in a rendered table (HTML comment,
# invisible in a markdown viewer). A resume / re-finalize that re-reads a report
# already carrying this marker is a no-op.
TABLE_MARKER = "<!-- polaris:summary_table -->"

# --- env kill-switch (default ON; LAW VI) -----------------------------------
_ENABLE_FLAG = "PG_RENDER_SUMMARY_TABLE"
_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})

# --- disclosed-gap marker ---------------------------------------------------
GAP_CELL = "—"

# --- excerpt caps -----------------------------------------------------------
_CLAIM_CELL_MAX_CHARS = 220
_LITERATURE_TITLE_MAX_CHARS = 60
_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL = 3

# --- provenance / citation tokens to strip from a claim sentence ------------
_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
_NUM_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")

@dataclass
class SummaryTableResult:
    text: str
    changed: bool
    canary: str = ""
    rows: int = 0
    headers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enablement / parsing helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def summary_table_enabled() -> bool:
    """LAW VI kill-switch (default ON). OFF => no table => byte-identical report."""
    return os.environ.get(_ENABLE_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES


def _smart_titlecase(phrase: str) -> str:
    """Title-case an ALL-lowercase header phrase (keeping connector words lowercase after
    the first word); a phrase that ALREADY carries any uppercase letter is returned
    VERBATIM (so "Country/Region" / "Specific Applications and Impacts" are preserved
    byte-for-byte and never mangled by a naive ``str.title``). PURE."""
    phrase = phrase.strip()
    if not phrase or any(c.isupper() for c in phrase):
        return phrase
    words = phrase.split()
    out: list[str] = []
    for i, w in enumerate(words):
        if i > 0 and w in _UNQUOTED_HEADER_SMALL_WORDS:
            out.append(w)
        else:
            out.append(w[:1].upper() + w[1:])
    return " ".join(out)


def parse_requested_headers(research_question: str) -> list[str]:
    """The exact column headers the prompt requests, or ``[]`` when the prompt does
    not ask for a titled summary table. Detection is CUE-anchored (a "column
    headers"/"columns should be"/"with columns:" phrase) so ordinary quoted text
    elsewhere in the prompt is never mistaken for a header list. Handles smart and
    straight quotes, then an UNQUOTED comma-list fallback (I-wire-001 F1). PURE."""
    if not research_question:
        return []
    cue = _HEADER_CUE_RE.search(research_question)
    if not cue:
        return []
    # Window from the cue to the end of its sentence/paragraph (bounded).
    start = cue.end()
    tail = research_question[start:start + 800]
    # Stop at a hard paragraph break if one occurs inside the window.
    para = tail.find("\n\n")
    if para != -1:
        tail = tail[:para]
    smart = [h.strip() for h in _SMART_QUOTED_RE.findall(tail) if h.strip()]
    if len(smart) >= 2:
        return smart
    straight = [h.strip() for h in _STRAIGHT_QUOTED_RE.findall(tail) if h.strip()]
    if len(straight) >= 2:
        return straight
    # I-wire-001 F1 — UNQUOTED fallback (belt-and-suspenders; the CONTRACT path is
    # primary). The cue matched but quote-extraction found < 2 header names, so the
    # prompt named the headers as a plain comma list ("... with columns: A, B, C, and
    # D."). Bound to the cue's SENTENCE, split on ',' ONLY (a bare " and " lives inside a
    # real header), drop a leading ", and", strip a trailing period, smart-title-case.
    sentence_end = _UNQUOTED_SENTENCE_END_RE.search(tail)
    sentence_tail = tail[: sentence_end.start()] if sentence_end else tail
    unquoted: list[str] = []
    for raw in sentence_tail.split(","):
        piece = _UNQUOTED_LEADING_AND_RE.sub("", raw.strip()).strip().rstrip(".").strip()
        if piece:
            unquoted.append(_smart_titlecase(piece))
    if len(unquoted) >= 2:
        return unquoted
    return []


def _classify_header(header: str) -> str:
    low = header.strip().lower()
    for role, keywords in _ROLE_KEYWORDS:
        if any(kw in low for kw in keywords):
            return role
    return ROLE_GAP


def assign_header_roles(headers: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Map each requested header to a column ROLE, then guarantee EXACTLY ONE
    claim-role column carries the verified sentence: if several headers classify as
    CLAIM only the first keeps it (the rest become disclosed-gap columns); if none
    classify as CLAIM the last non-literature column is promoted so the verified
    finding always has a home. PURE."""
    roles = [_classify_header(h) for h in headers]
    claim_idxs = [i for i, r in enumerate(roles) if r == ROLE_CLAIM]
    if claim_idxs:
        for i in claim_idxs[1:]:
            roles[i] = ROLE_GAP
    else:
        # Promote the last non-literature column (or the last column) to CLAIM.
        promote = None
        for i in range(len(roles) - 1, -1, -1):
            if roles[i] != ROLE_LITERATURE:
                promote = i
                break
        if promote is None and roles:
            promote = len(roles) - 1
        if promote is not None:
            roles[promote] = ROLE_CLAIM
    return roles

# [ev_XXX] marker and is re-checked by the UNCHANGED strict_verify, so the
    # research_question text can never enter the report as an unsupported claim.
    # Empty (the caller default) => byte-identical to the prior placeholder.
    _rq = (research_question or "").strip()
    _rq_line = (
        f"Research question (framing only — do NOT cite it as a source): {_rq}\n\n"
        if _rq
        else "Research question context: (see overall corpus)\n\n"
    )
    # LEVER 1 (render-blocks): the first-pass instruction says "paragraph" (singular); when the flag is
    # on ask for blank-line-separated paragraphs so the draft actually carries breaks. OFF => exact
    # original string, byte-identical.
    _final_write_line = (
        f"Write the {section.title} section now, organizing the body into paragraphs of 3 to 6 "
        f"sentences separated by a blank line, following the rules."
        if _render_blocks_enabled()
        else f"Write the {section.title} paragraph now, following the rules."
    )
    prompt = (
        f"{_rq_line}"
        f"Evidence available for this section ({len(evidence_subset)} rows):\n\n"
        f"{evidence_section}\n\n"
        f"{_final_write_line}"
    )

# LEVER B: build the per-URL RQ-SOURCE-ELIGIBILITY demote map — the task-derived
    # generalization of the scope/date WEIGHT seam above, driven by the constraints the RQ
    # ITSELF stated (source_types / languages / recency, parsed generically by
    # constraint_extractor and cached on protocol['_rq_constraints']). Gated
    # PG_RQ_SOURCE_ELIGIBILITY_ENFORCE (default OFF) inside build_rq_eligibility => an empty
    # plan => `url_to_eligibility_weight` empty + `_ineligible_urls` empty => the sort key +
    # tail partition are BYTE-IDENTICAL. An RQ-ineligible source (wrong source-type / non-English
    # / out-of-recency) is DEMOTED and sinks LAST while staying KEPT (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER);
    # unresolved rows (unknown genre/language/date) are fail-open (weight 1.0) + flagged for
    # fetch recovery. No post-generation gate is added (upstream-only).
    url_to_eligibility_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
    _ineligible_urls: set[str] = set()
    _elig_recovery_urls: set[str] = set()
    _elig_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.rq_eligibility import (  # noqa: PLC0415
            build_rq_eligibility,
        )
        _elig_plan = build_rq_eligibility(protocol, evidence_rows, research_question)
        url_to_eligibility_weight = dict(_elig_plan.url_to_eligibility_weight)
        _ineligible_urls = set(_elig_plan.ineligible_urls)
        # LEVER B recovery/disclosure surface: an UNRESOLVED row (unknown genre/language/date) is
        # KEPT at full weight but flagged so the operator can fetch-recover the missing signal;
        # the per-source demote records disclose exactly why each ineligible row was demoted.
        # These are consumed on EVERY selection path below (floor + capped), including the
        # unresolved-ONLY plan (no demotes, but recovery flags present).
        _elig_recovery_urls = set(_elig_plan.fetch_recovery_urls)
        _elig_records = list(_elig_plan.eligibility_records)
        if url_to_eligibility_weight:
            _LOGGER.info(
                "[select] LEVER B: DEMOTED %d RQ-ineligible source(s) "
                "(kept, sorted last — §-1.3 disclose, NOT dropped); reasons=%s",
                len(url_to_eligibility_weight),
                [r.get("reasons") for r in _elig_records][:10],
            )
        if _elig_recovery_urls:
            _LOGGER.info(
                "[select] LEVER B: FLAGGED %d source(s) for fetch-recovery "
                "(unknown genre/language/date — KEPT at full weight, disclosure only)",
                len(_elig_recovery_urls),
            )
    except Exception as _elig_exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - fail-open: eligibility never aborts selection
        _LOGGER.warning("[select] RQ eligibility skipped (%s)", _elig_exc)
        url_to_eligibility_weight = {}
        _ineligible_urls = set()

## 5. THE DEEPEST THING STILL WRONG

**Step 3's flagship lever is described as "turn on the existing section-structure path" — but only the prompt half of that path exists. The render half was never built.** The config's own comment proves it: PG_RENDER_BLOCKS exists precisely because the resolver flattens sections, and its block-preserving join handles *blank-line paragraph breaks only* — "paragraphs ONLY — no headings/tables/bullets, avoiding the PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE prompt conflict" (config_defaults.py:860-866). The resolver rebuilds every section from per-sentence strings joined with `" "` (provenance_generator.py:5239); there is **no code path that carries `###` lines, bullet lines, or GFM table rows through verify→resolve**. So arming PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE will make the writer emit structure and the resolver will mangle it into a blob — the replay-validate the plan prescribes will *fail*, and the plan specifies no fix for that failure. The fix must be built: either Sol's ReportBlock carriage through resolution/assembly, or a line-aware resolver join — or route all structure through the one flatten-safe seam that already exists, the **post-resolve appended-block seam the synthesis matrix uses** (config_defaults.py:867-871: "The table is APPENDED as a block (prose untouched)"). The plan should be amended to say this explicitly: in-prose structure requires new resolver code; deterministic tables should enter via the appended-block seam, which is safe today.
